x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces the Manhattan Borough President's open reference data (community boards, ULURP recommendations, appointments, funding awards, legislation, constituent cases) and adds apply_to_community_board — the net-new write workflow that today exists only as a WordPress/Forminator form.

server.name: io.nyc.manhattanbp
server.title: Manhattan Borough President (MBPO)
server.description: Agent-native access to the Office of the Manhattan Borough President — the borough's 12 community boards, ULURP land-use recommendations, Borough President appointments, discretionary/capital funding awards (the per-year Socrata datasets unified), legislation, and de-identified constituent-service cases from NYC Open Data, plus the net-new ability to apply to serve on a community board — an action that today lives only in a WordPress form.
server.instructions: Use find_community_boards / get_community_board to locate Manhattan's 12 boards (chair, district manager, meeting schedule, office). find_land_use_recommendations returns the BP's ULURP advisory recommendations (the recommendation body is a linked PDF). find_board_appointments lists people the BP has appointed to boards and councils. find_funding_awards answers 'who did the Manhattan BP fund?' across capital, tourism, community, MCAP, and police-community award programs and every fiscal year in one query — the underlying Open Data is split into one dataset per program per year. find_legislation covers policy positions; find_constituent_cases returns DE-IDENTIFIED case data only (never a constituent's identity). apply_to_community_board (net-new) submits an application to serve on a community board; it requires an authenticated applicant and mutates state — confirm the preferred district, connection to the district, and statement of interest with the user before submitting.

tools.name: find_community_boards
tools.title: Find Community Boards
tools.description: List Manhattan's 12 community boards with chair, district manager, meeting schedule, and office location.

tools.name: get_community_board
tools.title: Get Community Board
tools.description: Fetch a single Manhattan community board by district number (1–12).

tools.name: find_land_use_recommendations
tools.title: Find Land Use Recommendations
tools.description: Search the Borough President's ULURP advisory recommendations by application number, project, or date. The recommendation body is a linked PDF.

tools.name: find_board_appointments
tools.title: Find Board Appointments
tools.description: List people the Manhattan Borough President has appointed to boards, councils, and commissions, by board category or organization.

tools.name: find_funding_awards
tools.title: Find Funding Awards
tools.description: Search the Manhattan BP's discretionary, capital, tourism, community, MCAP, and police-community funding awards across every fiscal year in one query — the Open Data is split into one dataset per program per year.

tools.name: find_legislation
tools.title: Find Legislation
tools.description: List legislation and policy positions the Manhattan Borough President advocates, by status or subject.

tools.name: find_constituent_cases
tools.title: Find Constituent Cases
tools.description: Search DE-IDENTIFIED constituent-service cases (type, subject, status, geography) handled by the office. Never returns a constituent's identity.

tools.name: list_my_applications
tools.title: List My Applications
tools.description: List the authenticated applicant's own community-board applications and their status.

tools.name: apply_to_community_board
tools.title: Apply to a Community Board
tools.description: Submit an application to serve on a Manhattan community board. NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — it lives only in a WordPress/Forminator web form or a downloadable PDF. Requires an authenticated applicant. Mutates state — confirm the preferred district, connection to the district, and statement of interest first.
